Every bullet in your magazine matters.

A top-down roguelite arena shooter.

sardin-ventures/publisher pitch/demo on steam/2026

sardin-ventures

Independent studio
Based in France

We're sardin-ventures, two French brothers and software engineers by trade who love indie games; one of us previously worked at Nadeo, the studio behind TrackMania. We started RUNZ to build a fun experience worth sharing. It's our second game; the first is where we learned the craft, and this time we're set on delivering a polished, finished experience.

The early signs are good: our first playtesters really enjoyed it and played far more than we expected, and organic players are sticking with the Steam demo too. We're confident the game is genuinely fun and engaging. What we're missing is reach. We're developers, not marketers, and getting people to actually try it is where we struggle.

175unique demo players
35MINmedian playtime
120wishlists, all organic

Screenshots don't do it justice. RUNZ is best in motion.

Play the demo Watch the trailer ~5 min on Steam

Build your own magazine

RUNZ is a top-down arena shooter with a build layer borrowed from auto-battlers. You survive timed waves, dodging dense enemy bullet-hell, then reroll the shop to buy bullets and fill your magazine however you like. Bullets have multiple synergies that will grant powerful effects when activated.

The bullets in the magazine are read sequentially when firing. Synergies, mods, and enchants will dictate the overall theme of a run.

Roughly: Brotato's arena pressure meets the drafting of TFT and SNKRX.

Screenshots

The loop

A run is a mix of intense rounds and shop phases until you reach the boss.

1

Combat

Survive a timed wave. Aim, dodge, collect money and try to go for the heal.

2

Shop

Reroll to search for bullets, merge duplicate bullets to upgrade them, equip mods, enchant slots, level up or spend money on heals.

Money can feel scarce intentionally: rerolling, leveling, buying copies and saving for interest all compete for the same cash.

Assembling your magazine

Between waves you spend money to upgrade. Above the shop is your magazine, backpack, equipped mods and current synergies. The shop itself offers various services but the most important part is the random bullets that can be rerolled.

Bullets70+, each with stats and synergies.
Synergies24 unique synergies.
EnchantsStamp a chosen synergy straight onto a magazine slot, a second build axis that can tip a synergy over its next threshold or bridge into a new one.
Mods~48, from passive buffs to more elaborate ones that reward ordering bullets in specific ways.
Upgrades3-of-a-kind star-ups: the classic auto-battler upgrade mechanic.

Demo is up, core loop polished

StatusPublic demo live: the full combat-shop-combat loop, 20 rounds, boss encounter, win/lose flow.
PlatformsSteam: Windows, macOS & Linux. Mobile later, depending on Steam reception.
Content70+ bullets, 24 synergies, slot enchants, ~48 mods.
AudioA frenetic original soundtrack, composed entirely in-house.
Game feelJuicy, chunky feedback: enemies burst into flying parts, with screen shake, hit-stop and punchy impact VFX.
Meta systemsOnline leaderboards, a Codex collection, a first-run tutorial, character select.
LanguagesEnglish and French. Other languages are in the demo as rough machine translations, ready for a proper pass.
TeamGodot 4.6.2, single-player, built by 2 devs and self-funded on a steady release cadence.

Roadmap

Timeline is open to discussion. Most of the content pass could be done in as little as two to three months.

Shipped
done

Playable demo

The full run loop with 70+ bullets, 24 synergies, ~48 mods, a boss, leaderboards and FR/EN, live on Steam.

Current
in progress

Content & feel pass

More bullets, synergies, mods and characters, plus deeper progression and feel polish driven by demo feedback.

Next
planned

Wishlist & beta push

Store-page work, a trailer, streamer and press beats, a closed playtest cohort, and localization beyond FR/EN.

Goal
target

Full launch, then mobile

A paid 1.0 release with the deeper modes below in place, then mobile-friendly builds. The architecture was written with mobile in mind from the start.

On the roadmap

Characters

Unlockable loadouts that rewrite a run, such as a 3-slot magazine paired with 10 mod slots. Each one plays like a different game.

Achievement unlocks

New bullets, characters and mods earned by how you play, not only bought in the shop.

In-run unlock quests

Optional mid-run objectives that unlock a bullet or mod on the spot, guaranteed in your next shop. For example: play two rounds straight with no synergy active.

Ascension difficulty

Stacking modifiers for repeat runs that keep raising the ceiling once you've beaten it.

Endless mode

Drop the 20-round cap and push a build as far as it will go, chasing the leaderboard.

New stages

Fresh levels with their own layouts and hazards to fight across.

More bosses

Additional end-of-run encounters beyond the current one.

In-wave events & secrets

Random events and hidden surprises that can fire mid-wave and shake a run up.

What we're looking for

We're not looking for funding. Development is handled and self-financed. What we don't have is reach. That's the part we'd want a publisher for.

Playtesting

A real tester pipeline to pressure-test balance, difficulty and new-player onboarding at a scale one team can't reach.

Streamer & creator outreach

Intros and key distribution to creators whose audiences fit roguelite, arena and deckbuilder play.

Marketing & wishlists

Store-page craft, capsule and trailer feedback, launch timing, and a coordinated wishlist campaign.

Localization

We ship English and French ourselves. Other languages currently rely on rough machine translations; we'd love proper localization for them.

Terms

Up front
No advance, no development budget required.
Revenue
A revenue share on sales, open to whatever split or structure works best for both sides.
Scope
Which services above you take on is flexible.

Get in touch

The demo is the best way to get a feel for the game. If you like it, we'd love to hear from you.
